name: SDLC Baseline Live Test Cycle
description: Test-owned SDLC playbook used for repeatable live workflow proofs.
outcome: Production-ready software with tests and documentation.

definition.process_instructions: The live-test product manager clarifies the goal and acceptance criteria. The live-test architect produces the technical design before implementation. The live-test developer implements the change. The live-test reviewer reviews every developer-delivered change and sends rework back when needed. The live-test QA role validates evidence after review approval. This baseline flow has no human approval gates. When a checkpoint deliverable is accepted and the next checkpoint begins, complete the finished checkpoint work item instead of leaving it open.

columns.label: Planned
columns.description: Work is defined but not yet assigned.

columns.label: In Review
columns.description: Primary work is complete and under review.

columns.label: Blocked
columns.description: Work is blocked and needs intervention.

columns.label: Done
columns.description: The work item meets its acceptance criteria.

stages.name: requirements
stages.goal: Approved requirements with testable acceptance criteria

stages.name: design
stages.goal: Approved system design with key decisions captured

stages.name: implementation
stages.goal: Working code with tests on a feature branch

stages.name: review
stages.goal: Reviewer verdict with concrete findings or approval

stages.name: release
stages.goal: Operator-ready release package and final release summary

checkpoints.name: requirements
checkpoints.goal: Approved requirements with testable acceptance criteria.
checkpoints.entry_criteria: The workflow goal and initial context are available.

checkpoints.name: design
checkpoints.goal: A design exists for the implementation approach when needed.
checkpoints.entry_criteria: Requirements are clear enough to shape implementation.

checkpoints.name: implementation
checkpoints.goal: Working code exists and is ready for formal review.
checkpoints.entry_criteria: Requirements and any required design are available.

checkpoints.name: review
checkpoints.goal: A reviewer verdict exists for every developer-delivered change.
checkpoints.entry_criteria: Implementation output and developer handoff are available.

checkpoints.name: verification
checkpoints.goal: QA validation and evidence are complete.
checkpoints.entry_criteria: Required review is complete and approved.

checkpoints.name: release
checkpoints.goal: Release notes, residual risk, and operator summary are complete.
checkpoints.entry_criteria: Verification evidence and operator summary are ready.
